title: magnolia virginiana subsp. virginiana
intro: Magnolia virginiana subsp. virginiana, also known as sweet bay magnolia or southern sweet bay, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to eastern North America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, derived from its bark, leaves, and roots. The health benefits of Magnolia virginiana subsp. virginiana include reducing anxiety, stress, and inflammation, as well as acting as a natural antihistamine and expectorant. Magnolia virginiana subsp. virginiana can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments.
